compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCOPE DECISION (during implementation): the same-tracker race test surfaced a real TOCTOU bug in the parent-cascade (src/rebar/_commands/claim.py): the cascade decides to claim the parent on an UNLOCKED stale 'open' read; under a genuine race the second agent's locked parent claim raises ConcurrencyMismatch and the whole CHILD claim aborts with exit 10 -- so 'both children end claimed' (this story's AC) and the documented single-agent contract ('parent already in_progress -> only the requested ticket moves') are BOTH violated. Fixing claim.py in scope (adds it to file_impact): when the cascaded parent claim fails but the parent is no longer open (a peer progressed it), that is benign -> proceed to claim the child; only a still-open parent aborts. This makes the AC true and aligns the race with documented intent. RED-before-GREEN: corrected same-tracker test authored to assert both children claimed (RED on current code), then fix.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The parent-first claim/transition cascade is thoroughly covered for the sequential,
single-process case (parent-first, multi-level, already-progressed/blocked parent,
parent-failure-aborts-child, child-failure-after-parent, cycle termination, exit-code
propagation). There is no test for a cross-process / two-clone race where two agents
work different children of the same open parent. The report is right that the expected
semantics must be written down before a test can assert them (e.g. may children be
claimed independently once the parent is in progress? does a losing parent claim after
merge invalidate the corresponding child claim?).

## What

Committed policy (to be documented, then tested) for two same-parent/different-child
sub-cases:
- **(a) Same-tracker, serialized:** agent A claims the parent (cascade) then child A.
  Agent B, seeing the parent already `in_progress`, does NOT cascade and may claim
  child B independently under its own assignee. Parent ownership does NOT constrain
  sibling child assignees. This is fail-fast: if B's parent-claim were attempted while
  the parent is `open` and lost, B's child is never attempted.
- **(b) Two offline clones:** both independently claim the (open) parent + a child. On
  merge, forks are resolved **independently per ticket** by the reducer's UUID tie-break:
  - The PARENT's STATUS fork resolves to one winner (surfaced as `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED`
    on the parent); the losing agent's parent ownership is thereby signaled.
  - If both clones claimed the SAME child, the CHILD also has a concurrent STATUS fork,
    resolved by the same tie-break INDEPENDENTLY of the parent — so the child's winner may
    differ from the parent's winner. If they claimed DIFFERENT children, each child simply
    carries its own single claim.
  - In all cases the losing agent's already-written claim (parent and/or child) is **left
    in place (orphaned under the losing assignee); it is NOT retroactively rolled back or
    tombstoned.** The signal is `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED` on the affected ticket(s).

Document this in `docs/concurrency.md`/`CLAUDE.md`, then encode it in two tests.

## Scope

- `docs/concurrency.md` and/or `CLAUDE.md` (policy statement)
- `tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py` (or sibling) — new race tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The ownership policy for BOTH sub-cases above is written down in
      `docs/concurrency.md`/`CLAUDE.md`, including the explicit rule that a losing parent
      claim leaves the losing agent's child claim in place (orphaned), detected via the
      parent's `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED`.
- [ ] Same-tracker process race test (sub-case a): two real processes race on one
      tracker; confirms lock serialization, that B does not cascade a parent already
      `in_progress`, and both children end claimed under their respective assignees.
- [ ] Two-clone offline race test (sub-case b): both clones claim parent+child offline,
      then merge; confirms deterministic convergence, the parent `STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED`
      signal, that a SAME-child claim produces an independently-resolved child STATUS fork
      (child winner may differ from parent winner), and that the losing agent's claim(s)
      remain in place (not rolled back).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

file_impact.reason: cascade TOCTOU fix: benign parent-already-progressed proceeds to claim child

compiled_state.title: Define parent-cascade race policy + cross-clone race tests
